what_it_does: This file registers the GitHub CLI (`gh`) as a data-only platform in the Mirror-Registry System. It defines a single `PLATFORM` dictionary that tells the engine how to locate the binary, discover its flags and subcommands from `--help` output, read its version, classify its invariants/hazards/capabilities, and which projection targets it supports — all without defining any functions or classes.
observations: The entire file is one `PLATFORM = {...}` dict plus imports and a long docstring/comment; no `def` or `class`
It reuses the existing `cli-help` discovery type with `format: commander-options-text` and `parse_rule: option-row`
Discovery uses `gh pr create --help` as the primary option-table source and `gh --help` as a subcommand-list source
It sets `floor_guard: 10` on the option-table source and `floor_guard: 3` on the subcommand source
Transport invariants are declared explicitly (`--json`, `--jq`, `--template`, `--no-color`) rather than derived from a spawn head
There is no `head_builder` or held-open session machinery; `gh` is invoked as a one-shot subprocess
The signal-sets include hazard name vocabulary (`force`, `delete`, `yes`, `confirm`) and capability axes (`repo-target`, `browser`)
The docstring frames this file as a generalization proof: a second real CLI platform added with zero engine/adapter edits
standouts: The `floor_guard: 10` on `gh pr create --help` is a hard acceptance threshold meant to fail loudly on parser regressions
Transport invariants are labeled as `declared` because `gh` has no held-open spawn head
The file is intentionally pure data to mirror the `claude_code.py` template shape
concerns: The `commander-options-text` format assumption may not perfectly match Cobra's help layout for all `gh` versions
A fixed `floor_guard: 10` could break on versions where `gh pr create --help` emits fewer or differently formatted flags
The `parse_rule: subcommand-list` is declared but its parsing semantics are not visible in this file
Declared transport invariants are not verified by a spawn head, so drift between the declared list and actual binary behavior is possible
conventions: Row-purity: platform files contain only data, no executable code symbols
Stable selector dispatch via `DiscoverySource.type == "cli-help"` rather than platform-specific logic
Heavy inline documentation framing the file's role in the registry system
Data-driven executable location with env override, PATH fallback, and known paths
notes: A pure-data platform registration entry that uses the generic CLI-help adapter to onboard `gh` without changing the discovery engine.
questions: Does the `commander-options-text` parser handle Cobra's multi-line flag descriptions correctly?
What happens when `gh pr create --help` output changes and drops below the `floor_guard: 10` threshold?
How does the `subcommand-list` parse rule extract subcommands from `gh --help`?
Is the hazard vocabulary intended to match only whole flag names or substrings?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file explicitly states it is a generalization-proof data-only registration for `gh` and the content is exactly that.
intention_signals: The file is trying to demonstrate that adding a new CLI platform to the registry is 'almost free' — just a data dict reusing existing discovery and classification machinery, with no engine or adapter changes.
novelty: It deliberately chooses a real, non-stub CLI from a different tool family (Cobra-based `gh`) to prove the lift, and it does so as a pure-data row with no code symbols.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, self-contained example of how a subprocess-only CLI platform can be registered entirely through declarative data, including discovery commands, version probing, signal sets, and projection targets.
summary_for_embedding: A pure-data platform registry entry for the GitHub CLI that configures discovery from `gh pr create --help` and `gh --help`, version probing, declared transport invariants, hazard vocabulary, capability axes, and projection targets.
intention_for_embedding: To register `gh` as the second real CLI platform in the Mirror-Registry System using only declarative data and the existing cli-help adapter, proving the generalization lift.
